content.question: To what degree do early academic tracks (age 10/12) in Hungary directly cause between-school variance and SES/Roma segregation, versus serving as a selection mechanism that amplifies pre-existing residential or family-background segregation?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a matched-sample quasi-experimental study comparing students who narrowly qualified for versus narrowly missed 6/8-year gimnázium admission, tracking their school intakes and peer composition; or implement instrumental-variable estimation using school-choice lottery data or administrative discontinuities at selection-score thresholds
